name: claude-codex-settings
description: A personal Claude Code and OpenAI Codex setup with battle-tested plugins including skills, commands, hooks, agents, and MCP server configurations for Azure, GCloud, GitHub, Slack, Supabase, MongoDB, Tavily, Playwright, Linear, and more.

findings_summary.notes: Comprehensive, well-organized multi-plugin configuration with 15+ plugins. Each plugin has a proper plugin.json manifest, SKILL.md files, commands, hooks, and MCP configurations. Hook analysis: enforce_rg_over_grep.py (PreToolUse on Bash) blocks grep/find in favor of ripgrep — benign linting hook. gh_pr_create_confirm.py (PreToolUse) parses 'gh pr create' commands and shows a confirmation dialog before execution — a safety feature. git_commit_confirm.py similarly adds confirmation before git commits. The hooks use exit code 2 to block tool calls, which is the correct hook API. sync_marketplace_to_plugins.py and validate-agent.sh are developer tools. No data exfiltration, no hardcoded secrets, no obfuscation. MCP configs reference well-known servers (Context7, GitHub MCP, Slack MCP, etc.). A validation GitHub Action checks plugin manifests. Apache-2.0 licensed with CONTRIBUTING.md and INSTALL.md. The .claude/settings-zai.json and .codex/config.toml appear to be personal proxy configs (ccproxy/LiteLLM) but contain no secrets. Excellent overall quality and transparency.
